SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE

Agency: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (USACE), Savannah District
Synopsis Number: W912HN-25-SS-0043
Title: Facility Operations and Maintenance Support Services – Fort Stewart and Hunter Army Airfield, Georgia
NAICS Code: 561210 – Facilities Support Services
Size Standard: $47.0M
Response Deadline: February 14, 2025, 4:00 PM ET

The U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Savannah District, is conducting market research to determine the availability of qualified SBA-certified HUBZone small business concerns capable of providing comprehensive facility operations and maintenance services at Fort Stewart and Hunter Army Airfield, Georgia. Services include HVAC maintenance, plumbing, electrical systems repair, grounds maintenance, pest control, and refuse collection across approximately 1,200 facilities.

Based on market research results, the Government anticipates issuing this requirement as a HUBZone set-aside in accordance with FAR 19.1305 if two or more responsible HUBZone small business concerns are identified that can perform the work at fair market prices. The HUBZone price evaluation preference in accordance with FAR 19.1307 may also apply.

Interested firms must submit capability statements addressing the following:

(a) Company name, CAGE code, DUNS/UEI number, and current SBA HUBZone certification status (include certification date and SBA case number);
(b) Relevant past performance on contracts of similar size, scope, and complexity performed within the last five (5) years, including contract numbers, dollar values, and points of contact;
(c) Description of technical capability and available resources;
(d) Confirmation of ability to meet bonding requirements up to $5,000,000.

This notice is for market research and planning purposes only and does not constitute a solicitation or commitment by the Government to issue a solicitation or award a contract. No reimbursement will be made for any costs associated with providing information in response to this notice.

C.4 VAAR 852.219-73 VA NOTICE OF TOTAL SET-ASIDE FOR CERTIFIED SERVICE-DISABLED VETERAN-OWNED SMALL BUSINESSES (JAN 2023) (DEVIATION)

(a) Definition. for the Department of Veterans Affairs, ''Service-disabled Veteran-owned small business concern or SDVOSB'':
(1) Means a small business concern—
(i) Not less than 51 percent of which is owned by one or more service-disabled Veterans or, in the case of any publicly owned business, not less than 51 percent of the stock of which is owned by one or more service-disabled Veterans or eligible surviving spouses (see VAAR 802.201, Surviving Spouse definition);
(ii) The management and daily business operations of which are controlled by one or more service-disabled Veterans (or eligible surviving spouses) or, in the case of a service-disabled Veteran with permanent and severe disability, the spouse or permanent caregiver of such Veteran;
(iii) The business meets Federal small business size standards for the applicable North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code identified in the solicitation document;
(iv) The business has been certified for ownership and control pursuant to 38 U.S.C. 8127, 13 CFR 128, and is listed as certified in the SBA certification database at https://veterans.certify.sba.gov/; and
(v) The business agrees to comply with VAAR subpart 819.70 and Small Business Administration (SBA) regulations regarding small business size, government contracting, and the Veteran Small Business Certification Program at 13 CFR parts 121, 125, and 128.

(b) General. In order for a concern to submit an offer and be eligible for the award of an SDVOSB set-aside or sole source contract, the concern must qualify as a small business concern under the size standard corresponding to the NAICS code assigned to the contract and be listed as an SDVOSB participant in the SBA certification database as set forth in 13 CFR 128.
(1) Offers received from entities that are not certified SDVOSBs and listed in the SBA certification database at the time of offer shall not be considered.
(2) Any award resulting from this solicitation shall be made to a certified SDVOSB listed in the SBA certification database who is eligible at the time of submission of offer(s) and at the time of award.

SECTION B – SUPPLIES OR SERVICES AND PRICES/COSTS

B.1 CONTRACT TYPE
This is a firm-fixed-price contract for the provision of annual calibration and preventive maintenance services for environmental monitoring equipment located at the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service, Patuxent Research Refuge, Laurel, Maryland.

B.2 TOTAL CONTRACT CEILING
The total not-to-exceed (NTE) amount for this contract, inclusive of the base period and all option periods, shall be $47,500.00. The Government is not obligated to exercise any option periods and shall not be liable for costs exceeding this ceiling without a formal modification executed by the Contracting Officer.

B.3 PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE
Base Period: October 1, 2026 – September 30, 2027
Option Year 1: October 1, 2027 – September 30, 2028
Option Year 2: October 1, 2028 – September 30, 2029

B.4 SCOPE OF WORK
The Contractor shall provide semi-annual calibration, preventive maintenance, and emergency repair services for a total of fourteen (14) ambient air quality monitoring stations and associated meteorological sensor arrays. Services shall include on-site instrument diagnostics, sensor replacement as needed, NIST-traceable calibration verification, and documentation in accordance with EPA Quality Assurance Handbook Volume II. The Contractor shall maintain a 48-hour response time for emergency service calls. All replacement parts shall be OEM-equivalent or better. The Contractor shall deliver calibration certificates and maintenance logs within five (5) business days of each service visit. AMENDMENT OF SOLICITATION / MODIFICATION OF CONTRACT
Contract No.: GS-00F-334DA / Order No. 47QFCA25F0009
Ordering Agency: U.S. General Services Administration, Public Buildings Service – Great Lakes Region (Region 5)

A. PURPOSE
This document constitutes the Statement of Work (SOW) for an independent assessment of indoor air quality (IAQ) conditions at the Everett McKinley Dirksen United States Courthouse, 219 South Dearborn Street, Chicago, IL 60604. Multiple tenant agencies have reported concerns regarding ventilation adequacy and elevated humidity levels on floors 8 through 14 of the facility.

B. TASKS
The Contractor shall perform the following:
  (1) Conduct baseline IAQ monitoring over a minimum five (5) consecutive business days using calibrated instrumentation for CO₂, temperature, relative humidity, total volatile organic compounds (TVOCs), and particulate matter (PM2.5).
  (2) Evaluate the existing HVAC system's outdoor air delivery rates against ASHRAE Standard 62.1-2022 requirements.
  (3) Review building automation system (BAS) trend data for the previous six (6) months.
  (4) Deliver a comprehensive written report with findings, risk characterization, and prioritized corrective action recommendations.

C. PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE
All work and final deliverables shall be completed within forty-five (45) calendar days from the effective date of the task order.

D. FUNDING
The total contract value for this task order is $55,000.00. This amount represents the maximum obligation of the Government. Costs exceeding this amount are not authorized and will not be reimbursed. The Contractor shall manage resources prudently and shall immediately notify the Contracting Officer if at any point projected costs are expected to approach the funded ceiling.

E. APPLICABLE STANDARDS
All testing methodologies shall conform to EPA, NIOSH, and ASHRAE published protocols. The Contractor must hold current ACAC Council-certified Indoor Environmental Consultant (CIEC) credentials.

The objective is to create a secure, seamless, and interoperable health information ecosystem that supports warfighters from the point of injury in austere, forward-deployed locations to definitive care.

Offerors shall propose an innovative and scalable deployment plan addressing two interconnected lines of effort as outlined below. The proposed approach shall address hardware and/or component installation and configuration across various networks, continuous patient data access, and comprehensive user training tailored to the operational environment and a diverse user audience. Additionally, the plan shall incorporate proactive risk mitigation strategies to address deployment complexities and ensure mission success.

1a) Federal EHR product modernization and deployment: This effort focuses on the full implementation of the Federal EHR at established, high-capacity treatment facilities.

Scope:
• One large accredited Outside Continental U.S. (OCONUS) medical center (250 beds), including two community hospitals (125 beds) and five associated out-patient clinics spread across multiple time zones with approximately 5,000 total users that includes foreign national employees and 50,000 beneficiaries.

7. EVALUATION AND BASIS FOR AWARD (LPTA)
Award will be made in accordance with FAR 13.106-2 using a Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection process. The Government intends to establish a single award with a responsible ISBEE vendor whose quotation is determined to be technically acceptable and offers the lowest evaluated price.

Under the LPTA process, quotations will not be ranked or scored. Instead, quotations will be evaluated on an acceptable/unacceptable basis for non-price factors. Award will be made to the vendor whose quotation:
• Is determined to be technically acceptable under all non-price factors,
• Is compliant with all solicitation requirements,
• Is determined responsible in accordance with FAR Part 9, and
• Offers the lowest evaluated price.

Quotations will first be evaluated for eligibility and compliance with all solicitation requirements. This review will include:
• Verification of ISBEE eligibility status
• Submission of the required IEE Representative Form
• Completion of all required representations and certifications
• Adherence to quotation preparation instructions
Quotations that fail to meet solicitation requirements or are determined noncompliant will be eliminated from further consideration.

1. Description of Services
The Hybrid Architecture and Development for Experimental Systems (HADES) contract is a procurement for prototype and operational satellite ground systems, satellite operations centers (SOCs), and support services. The contractor shall perform satellite ground system and satellite operations center engineering, development, integration, and sustainment, and associated support services primarily at, but not limited to, the Research, Development, Test, and Evaluation (RDT&E) Support Complex (RSC) at Kirtland Air Force Base, Albuquerque, New Mexico. The organization's goal is to accelerate mission design and ground integration, launch and on-orbit operations, ground system tests, and rapid fielding to provide reliable, low-cost access to space in support of the warfighter.

THE NGA SOFTWARE WAY ELEMENTS

To consistently track these metrics and realize the above intent, the rest of this document provides a set of elements NGA and contractor teams must meet while delivering software products. These metrics and elements, collectively referred to as "The NGA Software Way," ensure each software product has the people, processes, and tools in place to effectively operate at NGA. They apply to any team building a new product or modifying one already in service, whether making small changes, adding large new features, or creating entirely new products. The NGA Software Way applies to software products of all phases, including existing software products across the agency (not simply new products).

13. Iterate and Improve Continually
Products and services are never "finished." Getting real people using your product as early as possible, monitoring the product's performance, and making improvements throughout the lifetime of the product enable teams to adapt quickly. Making improvements means more than doing basic maintenance like fixing defects in code, deploying security patches, and keeping runbooks up to date; continual improvement means you can capture and respond to changes in user needs, technology, or government policy throughout the lifetime of the product.

Iteration isn't just for the early stages of a product's development; a team must be able to make substantial improvements throughout a product's lifetime. As product complexity grows, teams must focus on balancing paying down technical debt with new feature development, ensuring the product continues to be adaptable over time to respond rapidly to user needs.

3. Prioritize Rapidly Delivering Value to Users
Once user needs are deeply understood, the team must decide which of those needs to prioritize. To do this, the team must define a minimum viable product (MVP), either in written form or, ideally, as a set of wireframes, and then share it with key stakeholders, customers, and users. The MVP is the smallest actual deliverable that can be reviewed by real customers to get feedback on if it solves a need and to test assumptions, learn quickly, and decrease risk upfront. The product manager must break down the MVP into actionable work items that are easily understood by the entire team. Teams must gather feedback as early as possible using mockups or interactive prototypes. Each day a product isn't actively solving user needs in production increases the risk the team is building the wrong thing. Teams must prioritize rapidly getting a product to actual users in production to learn what works and how to successfully iterate; this first capability must be delivered in months, not years.

STATEMENT OF WORK
Data Innovations Instrument Manager and Auto-Verification System
Central Arkansas Veterans Healthcare System (CAVHS)
Little Rock, Arkansas

1. BACKGROUND: The Central Arkansas Veterans Healthcare System (CAVHS), Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine Service (PLMS), requires the brand-name Data Innovations (DI) laboratory management system/license for continued support and enhancement of the laboratory interface middleware system known as Instrument Manager (IM). Data Innovations' IM middleware allows data transmission between laboratory equipment and the Veterans Health Information Systems and Technology Architecture (VistA/CPRS).

Requirement: This SOW covers the ongoing maintenance and support of the current IM system, continuation of auto-verification workflow development, and implementation of laboratory intelligence (Lab Intelligence) capabilities to monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) in compliance with Electronic Health Record Modernization (EHRM) initiative requirements.

Data Innovations is the sole-source provider for this specialized middleware system and provides all maintenance, support, and professional services directly.

1.0 Objectives

The objectives of this program are to design and demonstrate affordable and producible hypersonic weapon concepts through a multi-vendor, phased research and development (R&D) activity. The primary objective of this activity is to explore the lowest cost solutions that are rapidly producible and meet the minimum required speed, range, and payload. This activity will integrate mature, innovative technologies from conceptual design to flight demonstration, with the intent to select and fly at least two vehicle concepts. To maximize program execution speed, and preserve flexibility for phased maturation, the program will be executed utilizing options to structure the phased sprints. The Government intends to award baseline contracts (with a base period/option for foundational work, e.g., kickoff, conceptual development, preliminary manufacturing plan, and risk assessment) to multiple qualified vendors through competitive selection.

Subsequent optional sprints (e.g. Sprint 1: PDR, Sprint 2: CDR, Sprint 3: Manufacturing and Prototyping Demonstration, Sprint 4: Flight Test) will be exercised via separate contract options. If sprint 4 is exercised and successful flight test is achieved, the contract may mature to include related within scope additions for producibility and test production and to conduct a manufacturing readiness assessment with an objective to demonstrate manufacturability and scalability toward twenty (20) all-up-rounds (AUR) per month for representative hardware, enabling affordable production for ground/flight testing, qualification, and/or incremental modular performance enhancements.

The Government may exercise some, none, or all optional tasks via unilateral task order modifications. The effort includes design maturation, prototyping, and demonstration, with milestones for reviews and potential selection for the optional sprints. The period of performance for the baseline contract ordering period is 48 months, with deliverables supporting potential technology transition. This effort does not include production or operational fielding.

Patent Rights

Unless the Performer shall have notified ARPA-H in writing that the Performer does not intend to retain title, the Performer shall retain the entire right, title, and interest throughout the world to each Subject Invention consistent with the provisions of this Article.

Section 4.0: SECURITY REQUIREMENTS

4.1 DOD 8570.01-M, INFORMATION ASSURANCE (IA) WORKFORCE IMPROVEMENT PROGRAM

The DOD 8570.01-M, Information Assurance (IA) Workforce Improvement Program, requires training and certification for IA duties performed. All contract employees performing IA functions must meet the requirements in accordance with DOD/DLA guidance. Additionally, all contract employees must be certified in operating systems on any system on which System Administration duties are performed; refer to C3.2.4.8.3 for further guidance. Proof of certification must be provided before Privileged access is granted; this includes, but is not limited to, system administration access, network administration access, router/switch access, firewall access, and IDS access.

Any person performing any IA function must attain certification at the level of complexity as a 1, 2, or 3 as specified in the 8570.01-M.

All documentation required for security certification will be maintained by both the Contractor and the Government. All contract employees will have acquired the necessary certification requirements, per the DOD 8570.1, throughout the entire period of performance of the contract.
